title: Track joint assembly
abstract: A track joint assembly (124) pivotally links together track links of a continuous track (120). To form the track joint assembly (124), the first track link (122) may include a male lug (180) projecting from an edge of the track link (122) that can be received between first and second side lugs (182, 184) projecting from the second track link (122). A floating bushing (210) may be disposed inside a lug bore (190) of the male lug (180) and is in lubricated contact with the male lug (180). To retain the lubricant (220), first and second seals (230, 232) are disposed at either side of the floating bushing (210). A pin (200) is inserted through both the floating bushing (210) and lug apertures (192, 194) disposed through the first and second side lugs (182, 184) respectively. The pin (200) may be in dry contact with the floating bushing (210) and is either in a clearance fit or transition fit so that it can rotated with respect to the floating bushing (210).
claims: Claims Claims 1. A track joint assembly (124) comprising: a first track link (122) including a male lug (180) projecting from a first edge (168) of the first track link (122), the male lug (180) including a lug bore (190) disposed therein; a second track link (122) including a first side lug (182) including a first lug aperture (192) and a second side lug (184) including a second lug aperture (1 4), the first side lug (182) and the second side lug (184) projecting from a second edge (169) of the second track link (122) and being spaced apart from each other; a floating bushmg (210) coaxially disposed in the lug bore (190) to float with respect to the male lug (1 0), the floating bushing (210) and the male lug (180) in lubricated contact with each other, the floating bushing (210) having disposed therein a bushing bore (216); a first fixed bushmg (240) fixedly disposed in the lug bore (190) at a first side of the floating bushing (210), the first fixed bushing (240) adapted to position the first seal (230) to seal against a first side of the floating bushing (210); a second fixed bushing (242) fixedly disposed in the lug bore (190) at a second side of the floating bushing (210), the second fixed bushing (242) adapted to position the second seal (232) to seal against a second side of the floating bushing (210), a pin (200) coaxially inserted through the bushing bore (190). 2. The track joint assembly (124) of claim 1, wherein the lug bore (190) and the floating bushing (210) are sized to provide a clearance (2 8) therebetween. 3. The track j oint assembly ( 12/4 ) of claim. 2, further comprising a lubricant (220) disposed in the clearance (218). 4. The track joint assembly (124) of claim 3, wherein the lubricant (220) is viscous grease. 5. The track joint assembly (124) of claim 1, further comprising a first collar (250) disposed in the first lug aperture (192) and a second collar (252) fixedly disposed in the second lug aperture (194). 6. The track joint assembly (124) of claim 5, wherein each of the first collar (250) and the second collar (252) include a collar aperture (254) adapted to receive the pin (200). 7. The track joint assembly (124) of claim 6, wherein the pin (200) includes a first pin end (260) and a second pin end (262), the first pin end (260) disposed in the first collar (250) and the second pin end (262) disposed in the second collar (252). 8. The track joint assembly (124) of claim. 2, wherein the first fixed bushing (240) includes a groove (244) to retain the first seal (230) and the second fixed bushing (242) includes a groove (244) to retain the second seal (232). 9. The track joint assembly (124) of claim 2, wherein the first seal (230) and the second seal (234) are selected from a group consisting of a lip seal, a mechanical seal, and an o-ring. 10. The track joint assembly (124) of claim 1, wherein the pin (20) is a solid pm (200).
